summary: The transaction ledger is consistently treated as the accounting source of truth.
evidence: Cash, positions, FIFO lots, and ledger entries are deterministically replayed from canonical dated transactions.
potential_impact: Creates reproducible balances and supports audit reconciliation.
recommendation: Retain ledger authority and add periodic replay-integrity checks to the validation checklist.
affected_displays_or_models: Cash; holdings; basis; performance; transaction history

summary: Confirmed portfolio valuation requires complete prices and uses one common closing date across active symbols.
evidence: The common date is the minimum of each held symbol's latest confirmed price date; missing symbols make the valuation incomplete.
potential_impact: Prevents understated totals and mixed-date portfolio values.
recommendation: Retain and expose the common valuation date prominently.
affected_displays_or_models: Selected Totals; portfolio cards; forecast starting value

summary: Portfolio accounting uses split-adjusted, non-dividend-adjusted closes while dividends remain ledger cash.
evidence: Benchmark comparison uses total-return data separately.
potential_impact: Avoids double counting portfolio dividends while permitting fair total-return benchmark comparison.
recommendation: Retain strict dataset adjustment separation and provenance.

summary: Daily gain and TWR remove designated external investor flows.
evidence: DailyGain equals value change less external flow; TWR geometrically chains the resulting daily return.
potential_impact: Appropriate measurement of investment performance independent of contributions and withdrawals.
recommendation: Retain after adding awards to external-flow classification.
affected_displays_or_models: Daily gain; Custom gain; TWR; Growth of $100

summary: All-time gain/loss does not consistently mean inception through today.
evidence: Market data acquisition and reconstruction can terminate at the applied master end date; live overlay is added only when that end date is today.
potential_impact: A historical Custom End can change a metric labeled All-time.
recommendation: Calculate all-time independently from inception through today. Keep Custom End limited to custom/range metrics.
affected_displays_or_models: All-time gain/loss; combined all-time; per-portfolio all-time

summary: Awards currently count as investment performance but the owner defines them as external contributions.
evidence: Award is absent from EXTERNAL_FLOW_KINDS and EXTERNAL_FLOW_KINDS/EXTERNAL_CASH_FLOW_KINDS equivalents.
potential_impact: Awards inflate gain/loss and TWR rather than being neutral investor flows.
recommendation: Add award to external-flow classification in reconstruction and analytics, and add regression tests.
affected_displays_or_models: All-time; Daily; Custom; TWR; Growth of $100; Alpha/Beta

summary: Insufficient history is displayed as zero return, zero volatility, and zero drawdown.
evidence: summary_metrics returns four zeros when fewer than two usable values exist.
potential_impact: Zero is a valid financial result and falsely implies evidence.
recommendation: Return null/unavailable and disclose the observation count.
affected_displays_or_models: Compare Total return; Annualized return; Volatility; Max drawdown

summary: Holding All time and Custom values are current-lot unrealized price measures, not ledger-aware performance.
evidence: The holding table values only current FIFO lots and excludes sold positions and most income/expense effects.
potential_impact: Users can incorrectly reconcile these fields to portfolio total gain/loss.
recommendation: Rename them Unrealized gain/loss and Current-lot custom price change, with explanatory captions.

summary: Holding Day can span more than one market session.
evidence: The calculation uses the last two available price observations without verifying consecutive completed sessions.
potential_impact: A data gap can make a multi-day move appear as one-day P/L.
recommendation: Require consecutive market sessions or display the two actual dates.

summary: Holding shares are displayed with zero decimals in the exact-holdings tables.
evidence: The calculation preserves fractional quantities but the Streamlit column formatter uses zero decimals.
potential_impact: Visible values may not reproduce market value or basis for fractional positions.
recommendation: Display enough decimal places when any fractional shares exist.

summary: Displayed Annualized Alpha is a market-model intercept without a risk-free-rate adjustment.
evidence: Daily alpha is the mean of asset return minus Beta times SPY return, compounded for 252 days.
potential_impact: It should not be interpreted as Jensen or CAPM alpha.
recommendation: Label it Annualized market-model alpha or disclose the zero-risk-free-rate assumption.

summary: The minimum 60 overlapping daily observations is a useful availability gate but a short estimation period.
evidence: Sixty sessions can produce unstable Beta and Alpha estimates during unusual regimes.
potential_impact: Risk statistics may move materially with the selected date range.
recommendation: Keep the hard minimum and add preferred one-year observation guidance or confidence diagnostics.

summary: Missing observations can make a multi-session price change enter volatility as one daily return.
evidence: Series drop missing values before percentage-change calculations in several analytics paths.
potential_impact: Annualized volatility may be distorted when coverage has gaps.
recommendation: Require consecutive-session returns for daily volatility or scale explicitly by elapsed sessions.
affected_displays_or_models: Compare volatility; hypothetical volatility; market context

summary: Monitor Contribution is the holding's share of net daily dollar P/L, not return or risk contribution.
evidence: It divides holding change by total net change and becomes undefined only when the denominator is exactly zero.
potential_impact: Values can be extreme when gains and losses nearly offset and the label is ambiguous.
recommendation: Rename Share of net daily P/L, keep dollar contribution, and define a near-zero denominator threshold.

summary: Real-time values are explicitly indicative, provenance-aware, and complete-or-unavailable at the portfolio level.
evidence: Price falls back from latest trade to midpoint to previous close, while freshness and provider fields remain visible.
potential_impact: This is appropriate for an IEX active-session overlay and avoids silent zero valuation.
recommendation: Retain source, feed, time, and freshness disclosures.
affected_displays_or_models: Monitor price; value; selected total; daily change

summary: Income metrics are based on realized ledger cash and are master-end aware.
evidence: Selected, YTD, and trailing-365 totals include dividends and interest; normalized quarterly income uses inclusive days.
potential_impact: Provides reproducible cash-income reporting.
recommendation: Retain; add a warning when normalizing very short ranges.

summary: The primary Forecast view is assumption-driven lognormal planning, not an empirical portfolio forecast.
evidence: It uses constant expected return and volatility, independent normal monthly shocks, fixed nominal contributions, and a fixed seed.
potential_impact: Results can understate skew, serial dependence, regime changes, and parameter uncertainty.
recommendation: Keep the planning disclaimer and avoid presenting target probability as objective likelihood.
affected_displays_or_models: Forecast percentile bands and target probability

summary: The simple forecast describes contributions as today's dollars but does not inflation-adjust future contribution amounts.
evidence: The same nominal monthly contribution is added throughout the horizon.
potential_impact: Real contribution purchasing power declines under inflation even though the caption suggests constant real dollars.
recommendation: Change wording to nominal contribution or explicitly escalate it with inflation.

summary: Scenario calculations are reproducible, assumption-explicit, and separated from stochastic forecasts.
evidence: Each run records model version, assumptions, coverage, and optionally dataset references.
potential_impact: Good foundation for validation and audit trails.
recommendation: Retain exact assumption and dataset persistence.

summary: Dividend-reduction stress assumes constant trailing-365 dividends for every year with no reinvestment or opportunity cost.
evidence: Impact equals negative trailing positive dividends times reduction rate times years.
potential_impact: Long-horizon impact is intentionally simple and may materially differ from an economic projection.
recommendation: Disclose constant nominal income and no compounding; consider an optional reinvestment sensitivity.

summary: Low-return sensitivity changes expected baseline return rather than the low_return stress parameter.
evidence: The UI supplies expected_return variations while the stressed scenario is computed from assumptions.low_return.
potential_impact: The sensitivity label can imply the stress return itself is changing when it is not.
recommendation: Vary low_return for the stressed result or explicitly label the varied baseline assumption.

summary: Hypothetical actions operate on copied cash and lots and cannot mutate the ledger or submit orders.
evidence: The analysis applies FIFO changes to copied state and persists a ledger baseline hash.
potential_impact: Strong safety separation for what-if analysis.
recommendation: Retain baseline staleness checks and explicit ticket-review boundary.

summary: Hypothetical Retirement success probability is not produced by the full account-aware retirement engine.
evidence: The simplified helper only models lognormal return, fixed nominal monthly spending, and terminal survival.
potential_impact: The displayed label can imply tax, account-type, income, inflation, and withdrawal analysis that did not occur.
recommendation: Rename Simplified depletion probability or integrate chat_alpaca.retirement.

summary: The full retirement request builder does not populate taxable cost basis from ledger FIFO lots.
evidence: RetirementAccount objects are created from portfolio values without taxable_cost_basis, causing the engine to use the realization-fraction fallback.
potential_impact: Estimated taxable withdrawal taxes can differ materially from ledger-informed embedded gains.
recommendation: Aggregate taxable FIFO basis at the common as-of date and pass it to each taxable RetirementAccount.
affected_displays_or_models: Retirement taxes; funding probability; terminal values

summary: The retirement engine applies the same portfolio return to every account.
evidence: A single portfolio return derived from household asset weights multiplies every account balance.
potential_impact: Tax location and account-specific allocations do not affect return or risk.
recommendation: Disclose the common-allocation assumption or model holdings and returns by account.

summary: Taxes are transparent flat-rate planning estimates rather than tax-law calculations.
evidence: The engine excludes brackets, deductions, filing status, RMDs, state/local taxes, loss harvesting, detailed future lots, and provisional-income rules.
potential_impact: Results should not be used as tax advice or precise tax liability estimates.
recommendation: Retain prominent limitations and sensitivity-test all rates.

summary: Outside income above current monthly spending is discarded.
evidence: Only min(net outside income, spending) is accumulated as used; surplus is not added to balances.
potential_impact: Scenarios with substantial pension or Social Security surplus may understate wealth.
recommendation: Disclose prominently or add an explicit reinvest-surplus assumption.
affected_displays_or_models: Retirement balances; target estate; outside-income contribution

summary: Annual contributions occur in model month zero and every twelfth pre-retirement month.
evidence: The condition month modulo 12 equals zero applies the annual amount at the beginning of each modeled year.
potential_impact: Timing differs from an end-of-year contribution assumption.
recommendation: Document timing or make timing owner-selectable.

summary: Historical block bootstrap preserves joint asset observations and short contiguous return sequences without imposing an expected return.
evidence: Circular blocks are sampled with a deterministic seed and explicit proxies are required for inadequate histories.
potential_impact: Method is reproducible and captures more empirical dependence than the simple forecast.
recommendation: Retain dataset, proxy, block-length, seed, and backtest evidence.

summary: Parametric estimates use cross-sectional return shrinkage, covariance shrinkage, matrix validation, and optional fat tails.
evidence: Expected returns derive from mean log returns and covariance is shrunk toward its diagonal.
potential_impact: More defensible than unregularized sample estimates.
recommendation: Retain parameter-source disclosures and validation evidence.

summary: A return override plus expected-return shift could potentially make a final annual return no greater than negative 100%.
evidence: The draw stage applies log1p to final annual returns; validation primarily constrains individual assumptions.
potential_impact: This can create invalid numerical values in extreme owner overrides.
recommendation: Validate every final blended-and-shifted annual return is greater than negative 100%.

summary: Fill synchronization applies only incremental provider fills and avoids duplicate ledger posting.
evidence: Incremental quantity and notional are calculated from provider cumulative totals less previously applied totals.
potential_impact: Supports idempotent synchronization and auditable order assignment.
recommendation: Retain integration tests and reconcile any future provider fees separately.

summary: Alpaca account and order fields are provider passthroughs rather than DashApp calculations.
evidence: Equity, cash, buying power, status, filled quantity, and average fill originate in provider responses.
potential_impact: They require timestamped source reconciliation instead of spreadsheet recomputation.
recommendation: Capture provider response time and compare exact raw values before UI rounding.

summary: Metric-specific validation tolerances are now documented in the audit.
evidence: Currency, shares, ratios, dates, provider values, and deterministic simulations have separate tolerances.
potential_impact: Prevents false failures from display rounding while preserving strict accounting checks.
recommendation: Apply tolerances to automated reconciliation tests and record raw versus displayed results.

summary: The audit inventory covers current calculated displays, stored/provider passthrough fields, approved methodology changes, and advanced retirement, tax, bootstrap, and parametric service outputs.
evidence: Each audit row includes a Google Sheets formula template, inputs, units, date scope, rounding, tolerance, missing-data behavior, assessment, target methodology, and source reference.
potential_impact: Provides a durable specification for later calculation edits without authorizing those edits.
recommendation: Review and approve audit rows before changing calculation code; implement critical items incrementally with regression tests.
